mistakes.question: According to the text, what is the Transformer architecture based on?
What does the Transformer dispense with?
What BLEU score did the Transformer achieve on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task?
On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, what BLEU score did the Transformer achieve?
The encoder maps an input sequence to a sequence of what?
How many identical layers are in the encoder stack of the Transformer?
What is the dimension of the outputs produced by all sub-layers and embedding layers in the model?
What is the main advantage of using the 'synchronized' keyword in Java?
mistakes.explanation: The main advantage of the 'synchronized' keyword is that it resolves data inconsistency problems when multiple threads operate on the same object.
mistakes.user_answer: It increases waiting time of threads

mistakes.question: What is the difference between 'notify()' and 'notifyAll()' in Java?
mistakes.correct_answer: 'notify()' notifies one waiting thread, while 'notifyAll()' notifies all waiting threads
mistakes.explanation: The 'notify()' method notifies one waiting thread, while the 'notifyAll()' method notifies all waiting threads.
mistakes.user_answer: 'notify()' notifies the thread with the highest priority, while 'notifyAll()' notifies all threads

remedial_plan.diagnosis: Concept Gap: Misunderstood the definition of 'synchronized' keyword and the behavior of 'notify()' and 'notifyAll()'
remedial_plan.explanation: The 'synchronized' keyword ensures that only one thread can access a resource at a time, preventing data inconsistency. 'notify()' wakes up one waiting thread, while 'notifyAll()' wakes up all waiting threads.

practice_question.question: Which of the following statements is true about the 'synchronized' keyword and 'notify()' method in Java?
options: A:'synchronized' increases waiting time; 'notify()' wakes up the highest priority thread
B:'synchronized' resolves data inconsistency; 'notify()' wakes up one waiting thread
C:'synchronized' decreases waiting time; 'notifyAll()' wakes up all waiting threads
D:'synchronized' is used for multi-threading; 'notify()' and 'notifyAll()' are not used together
practice_question.explanation: 'synchronized' resolves data inconsistency by ensuring exclusive access to a resource, and 'notify()' wakes up one waiting thread.
